8.title: 1. Rational Numbers
8.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Closure, Commutativity, and Associativity properties. Focus on Distributive law and finding numbers between two rationals.

8.title: 2. Linear Equations in One Variable
8.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Solving equations with variables on both sides. High priority: Word problems on ages, perimeters, and currency.

8.title: 3. Understanding Quadrilaterals
8.content:
◆ Marks: 7 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Interior/Exterior angle sum property. Focus on properties of Parallelograms, Rhombus, and Squares.

8.title: 4. Data Handling
8.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Grouped frequency distribution, Histograms, and Circle graphs (Pie Charts). Basic probability of events.

8.title: 5. Squares and Square Roots
8.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Square numbers properties, Pythagorean triplets. Must practice: Long Division method for decimals.

8.title: 6. Cubes and Cube Roots
8.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Properties of cube numbers. Prime factorisation method to find cube roots.

8.title: 7. Comparing Quantities
8.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Profit/Loss %, Sales Tax/GST. Most important: Compound Interest formula (Annually vs Half-yearly).

8.title: 8. Algebraic Expressions and Identities
8.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Multiplication of polynomials. Focus on Identities: (a+b)², (a-b)², and (a²-b²).

8.title: 9. Mensuration
8.content:
◆ Marks: 10 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Area of Trapezium and General Quadrilaterals. Surface Area and Volume of Cuboid, Cube, and Cylinder.

8.title: 10. Exponents and Powers
8.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Powers with negative exponents, Laws of Exponents. Expressing very small/large numbers in Standard Form.

8.title: 11. Direct and Inverse Proportions
8.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Direct proportion vs Inverse proportion. Focus on Time & Work and Speed-Distance problems.

8.title: 12. Factorisation
8.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Factorisation using Identities and Splitting the middle term. Division of algebraic expressions.

8.title: 13. Introduction to Graphs
8.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Linear graphs, independent vs dependent variables. Focus on reading coordinates (x, y) accurately.

9.title: 1. Number Systems
9.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Rationalisation of denominators, Laws of exponents for real bases. Focus on Representing √x on number line.

9.title: 2. Polynomials
9.content:
◆ Marks: 12 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Remainder and Factor Theorems. Practice 3-variable identities like (x+y+z)² and x³+y³+z³-3xyz.

9.title: 3. Coordinate Geometry
9.content:
◆ Marks: 4 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Cartesian plane, Quadrants, and plotting points. Understanding the origin (0,0).

9.title: 4. Linear Equations in Two Variables
9.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Standard form ax+by+c=0. Solving for x/y and plotting the straight-line graph.

9.title: 5. Introduction to Euclid’s Geometry
9.content:
◆ Marks: 2 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Euclid's 5 Postulates and Axioms. High priority: Statement of the 5th Postulate.

9.title: 6. Lines and Angles
9.content:
◆ Marks: 7 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Vertically opposite angles, Parallel lines with transversals, and Triangle angle sum property.

9.title: 7. Triangles
9.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Congruence rules (SAS, ASA, SSS, RHS). Inequalities in triangles and CPCT proofs.

9.title: 8. Quadrilaterals
9.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Properties of parallelograms and the Mid-point Theorem (very important for exams).

9.title: 9. Circles
9.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Equal chords and their distance from center. Angles subtended by arcs and Cyclic Quadrilateral properties.

9.title: 10. Heron’s Formula
9.content:
◆ Marks: 4 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Calculating triangle area using semi-perimeter(s). Application in finding quadrilateral areas.

9.title: 11. Surface Areas and Volumes
9.content:
◆ Marks: 10 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Surface area and Volume of Cones, Spheres, and Hemispheres. Focus on formula-based word problems.

9.title: 12. Statistics
9.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Bar graphs, Histograms (varying base), and Frequency Polygons. Mean, Median, and Mode of raw data.

10.title: 1. Real Numbers
10.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic. Proving √2, √3, √5 as irrational. HCF/LCM application.

10.title: 2. Polynomials
10.content:
◆ Marks: 4 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Geometrical meaning of zeroes. Relationship between zeroes and coefficients of quadratic polynomials.

10.title: 3. Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
10.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Graphical method, Substitution, and Elimination. High priority: Word problems on upstream/downstream and fractions.

10.title: 4. Quadratic Equations
10.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Solving by Quadratic Formula. Nature of Roots (Discriminant). Practice word problems based on speed and time.

10.title: 5. Arithmetic Progressions (AP)
10.content:
◆ Marks: 7 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Finding the n-th term (an) and Sum of first n terms (Sn). Focus on daily life applications of AP.

10.title: 6. Triangles
10.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Basic Proportionality Theorem (BPT) proof. Similarity criteria (AA, SAS, SSS) and Area of similar triangles.

10.title: 7. Coordinate Geometry
10.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Distance Formula, Section Formula, and Midpoint Formula. Focus on finding the Centroid.

10.title: 8. Introduction to Trigonometry
10.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Trig ratios of specific angles (30, 45, 60). Proving identities like sin²θ + cos²θ = 1.

10.title: 9. Some Applications of Trigonometry
10.content:
◆ Marks: 4 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Heights and Distances. Focus on multi-object problems with two angles of elevation/depression.

10.title: 10. Circles
10.content:
◆ Marks: 4 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Tangent to a circle theorem. Proving that tangents from an external point are equal.

10.title: 11. Areas Related to Circles
10.content:
◆ Marks: 4 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Area of sector and segment. Problems based on combinations of plane figures (circles + squares).

10.title: 12. Surface Areas and Volumes
10.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Surface area and Volume of combinations of solids (Cylinder + Cone + Hemisphere).

10.title: 13. Statistics
10.content:
◆ Marks: 7 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Mean (Direct & Assumed Mean), Median, and Mode. Understanding the Empirical relationship.

10.title: 14. Probability
10.content:
◆ Marks: 4 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Theoretical probability of events. Focus on Playing Cards, Dice, and Coin outcomes.

11.title: 1. Sets
11.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Empty, Finite/Infinite sets, Subsets. Operations: Union, Intersection, and Venn Diagrams.

11.title: 2. Relations and Functions
11.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Cartesian products, Domain, Co-domain, and Range. Understanding Identity, Modulus, and Signum functions.

11.title: 3. Trigonometric Functions
11.content:
◆ Marks: 10 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Radian measure, Sign of trig functions. Most important: Formulas like sin(x+y), cos 2x, tan 3x.

11.title: 4. Complex Numbers and Quadratic Equations
11.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Algebra of complex numbers, Modulus, and Conjugate. Solving quadratic equations with complex roots.

11.title: 5. Linear Inequalities
11.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Algebraic and Graphical solutions. Shading the correct feasible region on the graph.

11.title: 6. Permutations and Combinations
11.content:
◆ Marks: 7 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Fundamental principle of counting, Factorials. Use of nPr for arrangements and nCr for selections.

11.title: 7. Binomial Theorem
11.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Pascal's Triangle and expansion of (a+b)ⁿ. Focus on finding the general term and middle term.

11.title: 8. Sequences and Series
11.content:
◆ Marks: 7 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Arithmetic Mean (AM) and Geometric Progression (GP). Relationship between AM and GM.

11.title: 9. Straight Lines
11.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Various forms of line equations (Point-slope, Intercept). Distance between a point and a line.

11.title: 10. Conic Sections
11.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Standard equations of Circle, Parabola, Ellipse, and Hyperbola. Finding Focus, Directrix, and Eccentricity.

11.title: 11. Introduction to Three Dimensional Geometry
11.content:
◆ Marks: 4 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Coordinate axes/planes in 3D. Distance formula and Section formula in three dimensions.

11.title: 12. Limits and Derivatives
11.content:
◆ Marks: 10 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Limits of polynomials/rational functions. Practice 'First Principle' of differentiation for sin x, cos x, xⁿ.

11.title: 13. Statistics
11.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Measures of dispersion: Mean deviation, Variance, and Standard Deviation for grouped data.

11.title: 14. Probability
11.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Axiomatic probability, Events (OR, AND, NOT). Mutually exclusive and exhaustive events.

12.title: 1. Relations and Functions
12.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Reflexive, Symmetric, and Transitive relations. Bijective functions (One-to-One and Onto).

12.title: 2. Inverse Trigonometric Functions
12.content:
◆ Marks: 3 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Principal Value Branches. Focus on Domain and Range restrictions of sin⁻¹x, cos⁻¹x, etc.

12.title: 3. Matrices
12.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Easy
◆ Core Topics: Multiplication of matrices, Transpose. Symmetric and Skew-symmetric matrix properties.

12.title: 4. Determinants
12.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Area of a triangle, Adjoint, and Inverse. Solving linear equations using Matrix Method (X = A⁻¹B).

12.title: 5. Continuity and Differentiability
12.content:
◆ Marks: 9 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Chain rule, Implicit/Logarithmic differentiation. Second-order derivatives and Rolle’s Theorem.

12.title: 6. Application of Derivatives
12.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Rate of change, Increasing/Decreasing functions. 6-mark word problems on Maxima and Minima.

12.title: 7. Integrals
12.content:
◆ Marks: 10 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Integration by Substitution, Partial Fractions, and Parts. Definite integral properties (King's Property).

12.title: 8. Application of Integrals
12.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Area bounded by simple curves like Circles, Parabolas, and Ellipses using integration.

12.title: 9. Differential Equations
12.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Order and Degree. Variable separable method and Linear Differential Equations (dy/dx + Py = Q).

12.title: 10. Vector Algebra
12.content:
◆ Marks: 6 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Direction cosines, Unit vectors. Scalar (Dot) product and Vector (Cross) product applications.

12.title: 11. Three Dimensional Geometry
12.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Vector/Cartesian equations of a line. Shortest distance between two skew lines.

12.title: 12. Linear Programming
12.content:
◆ Marks: 5 | Difficulty: Medium
◆ Core Topics: Feasible region (bounded/unbounded). Corner Point method for finding Optimal Solutions.

12.title: 13. Probability
12.content:
◆ Marks: 8 | Difficulty: Hard
◆ Core Topics: Conditional Probability, Multiplication Theorem. Most important: Bayes' Theorem and Random Variables.
